title: Loops
description: Repeat actions with for and while loops.
objectives: Use while loops with a condition
Control loops with break and continue
recap: for loops iterate a fixed number of times using range(). while loops repeat until a condition becomes false. break exits early, continue skips to the next iteration.
practicePrompts: Can you make the loop count backwards from 10 to 1?
Try printing only the even numbers between 1 and 20 using a loop.

practiceExercises.title: Count down from 5
practiceExercises.prompt: Use a for loop with range() to print the numbers 5, 4, 3, 2, 1 each on their own line.
practiceExercises.goal: Print a countdown from 5 to 1 using a loop.
hints: range() takes three arguments: range(start, stop, step).

practiceExercises.title: Sum from 1 to 10
practiceExercises.prompt: Use a for loop to add the numbers 1 through 10 together, then print the total. Output should be exactly `55`.
practiceExercises.goal: Accumulate a running total inside a loop.
hints: Inside the loop: `total += i` (or `total = total + i`).

practiceExercises.title: Even numbers only
practiceExercises.prompt: Print the even numbers from 2 through 10, each on its own line. Use `continue` to skip odd numbers rather than stepping by 2.
practiceExercises.starterCode: # Print 2, 4, 6, 8, 10 — use continue to skip odd numbers
hints: Inside: `if i % 2 == 1: continue` — then print i.
`continue` jumps straight to the next iteration.
